messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: Bregman Projection for Calibration Estimation
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Bregman Projection for Calibration Estimation
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - This seminar offers valuable insights into robust methods for improving your data analysis and survey estimations. Bregman Projection for Calibration Estimation (Free Seminar) is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 2 with Wei Fu (Merck), Jae-Kwang Kim (Department of Statistics, Iowa State University), and Pushpal Mukhopadhyay (Energy Information Administration and Washington Statistical Society). This seminar introduces calibration weighting as a Bregman projection, offering a unified framework to integrate auxiliary information into survey estimation and mitigate selection bias, which is crucial for robust epidemiological studies. Researchers in epidemiology can apply these techniques to improve the accuracy and efficiency of their analyses, especially when dealing with complex survey data or high-dimensional covariates in R. The framework supports debiased prediction and doubly robust estimation, enabling more reliable inferences from observational and survey-based epidemiological data.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/bregman-projection-for-calibration-estim) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: Machine Learning for Computational Biology
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Machine Learning for Computational Biology
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - We invite you to explore an upcoming seminar on advanced analytical techniques relevant to your research. Machine Learning for Computational Biology 3.0 is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 14 with Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University). This seminar provides a comprehensive understanding of machine learning algorithms, including neural networks, random forests, k-means clustering, and MCMC methods. Participants will gain hands-on experience coding these algorithms from scratch using R and Python, focusing on practical applications for complex biological data. These skills can be directly applied by R-sig-Epi researchers to enhance their statistical analysis and display of epidemiological data, offering powerful new tools for their own projects.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/machine-learning-for-computational-biolo-2) to reserve your place before it starts, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: Causal Inference with LLMs: Apply to Your Data
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Causal Inference with LLMs: Apply to Your Data
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - Explore how recent advancements can transform your approach to complex data analysis and causal inquiry. Causal Inference with LLMs is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 8 and 10 with Moses Boudourides (Data Science Graduate Program, School of Professional Studies, Northwestern University). Epidemiologists often work with complex data, including textual and mixed-format datasets, where traditional causal inference methods can face challenges in measurement and discovery. This seminar introduces how large language models (LLMs) can be rigorously integrated into causal inference workflows, offering new possibilities for extracting, constructing, and analyzing variables from these diverse data sources. Through reproducible scripts in Python and R, researchers can gain practical skills to formalize causal questions in LLM-augmented designs, audit bias, and validate outputs, thereby strengthening their ability to produce publication-ready causal analyses in their own work.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/causal-inference-with-llms) to reserve your place before it starts, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] (Online course) Fundamentals of Biostatistics with R | 13–17 April
messages.subject_clean: (Online course) Fundamentals of Biostatistics with R | 13–17 April
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
it is still possible to join us for the upcoming online course, Fundamentals of Biostatistics with R, taking place from 13?17 April.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biostats-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biostats-in-r/ )
 
 
This course provides a comprehensive and practical introduction to the core statistical concepts and methods used in clinical and biomedical research. Through a hands-on approach in R, participants will learn how to explore, analyze, and interpret medical data, while gaining a solid understanding of key techniques such as hypothesis testing, ANOVA, regression models, and survival analysis.
 
Particular emphasis is placed on selecting appropriate statistical methods, correctly interpreting results, and ensuring reproducible and transparent data analysis in line with modern publication standards.
 
The course is suitable for researchers, clinicians, and students who wish to strengthen their statistical skills and improve the quality of their data analysis and reporting.
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )
